Coagulation

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and markedly increased total bilirubin, suggesting significant renal and hepatic dysfunction. There is mild hypoglycemia, low arterial pH (acidemia), and reduced oxygen saturation, indicating possible metabolic or respiratory compromise. Platelets are at the lower end of normal, and coagulation tests show a slightly prolonged aPTT and low PT. Further clinical correlation and urgent evaluation for underlying causes, including possible multi-organ involvement, are recommended.
